back_translation: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land of your dwellings which I am giving to you,

and you make a fire offering to the LORD, a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a special vow, or as a freewill offering, or in your appointed feasts, to make a pleasing aroma to the LORD, from the herd or from the flock —
then the one offering shall present his offering to the LORD: a grain offering, fine flour, a tenth (ephah) mixed with a quarter of a hin of oil.
And wine for a drink offering, a quarter of a hin, you shall prepare with the burnt offering or with the sacrifice, for each lamb.
Or for a ram you shall prepare a grain offering, fine flour, two tenths, mixed with oil, a third of a hin.
And wine for a drink offering, a third of a hin; you shall present a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
And when you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or sacrifice, to fulfill a special vow or a peace offering to the LORD,
and you shall present with the young bull a grain offering, fine flour, three tenths, mixed with oil, half a hin.
And wine you shall present for a drink offering, half a hin — a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Thus it shall be done for one bull, or for one ram, or for a lamb among sheep, or among goats.
According to the number which you prepare, thus you shall do for each one according to their number.
Every native shall do these things in this way, to present a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
And if a foreigner dwells with you, or whoever is in your midst throughout your generations, and he makes a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD — as you do, so he shall do.
The assembly — one statute shall be for you and for the foreigner who dwells — a permanent statute for your generations; as you, so the foreigner shall be before the LORD.
One law and one ordinance shall be for you and for the foreigner who dwells with you.
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land which I am bringing you there,
and it shall be when you eat of the bread of the land, you shall lift up a contribution to the LORD.
The first of your dough, a cake you shall lift up as a contribution; as the contribution of the threshing floor, so you shall lift it up.
From the first of your dough you shall give to the LORD a contribution throughout your generations.
And if you stray and do not do all these commandments which the LORD has spoken to Moses —
all that the LORD has commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day the LORD commanded and onward, throughout your generations —

and it shall be, if it was done unintentionally from the eyes of the congregation, then the whole congregation shall prepare one young bull as a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD, with its grain offering and its drink offering according to the ordinance, and a male goat as a sin offering.

And the priest shall make atonement for the whole congregation of the sons of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them; for it was unintentional, and they brought their offering, a fire offering to the LORD, and their sin offering before the LORD for their unintentional sin.
And it shall be forgiven to the whole congregation of the sons of Israel and to the foreigner who dwells in their midst, because to all the people in unintentional error.
And if one soul sins unintentionally, he shall present a female goat, a year old, as a sin offering.
And the priest shall make atonement for the soul who erred by sinning unintentionally, before the LORD, to make atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him.
The native among the sons of Israel and the foreigner who dwells in their midst — one law shall be for you, for the one who acts unintentionally.
But the soul that acts with a high hand, from the native and from the foreigner — that one is blaspheming the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from the midst of his people.
Because the word of the LORD he has despised, and His commandment he has broken — being cut off, that soul shall be cut off; his iniquity is upon him."
And the sons of Israel were in the wilderness, and they found a man gathering wood on the Sabbath day.
And those who found him gathering wood brought him to Moses and to Aaron and to the whole congregation.
And they placed him in custody, because it had not been declared what should be done to him.
And the LORD said to Moses: 'The man shall surely be put to death; the whole congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp.'
And the whole congregation brought him outside the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
And the LORD said to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: Make for yourselves tassels on the corners of your garments throughout your generations; and put on the tassel of the corner a blue cord.

And it shall be to you as tassels; and you shall look at it and remember all the commandments of the LORD, and you shall do them, and you shall not follow after your heart and after your eyes, after which you go astray (prostitute yourselves).
So that you shall remember and do all My commandments, and you shall be holy to your God.
I am the LORD your God, who brought you out from the land of Egypt to be your God; I am the LORD your God."
